title: Hey Ya!
literal: a lively party anthem about dancing and romantic attraction
trueSubject: the fragility and denial of modern relationships
subtext: The song masks deep skepticism about love's permanence behind an upbeat, danceable facade, suggesting people ignore relationship unhappiness to keep the party going.
scenario: a man performing at a club, outwardly celebrating love while internally questioning its sustainability

title: Pumped Up Kicks
literal: a troubled teen threatening violence with a gun
trueSubject: youth alienation and school shootings
subtext: The song masks a cry for help and a warning about the consequences of neglect and untreated mental health issues in adolescents.
scenario: a lonely, neglected teenager fantasizing about retaliation against peers who exclude or bully him

title: Semi-Charmed Life
subtext: Instrumental — no lyrics to interpret.

title: Every Breath You Take
literal: a lover obsessively monitoring their partner's actions
trueSubject: coercive control and emotional abuse
subtext: The narrator masks possessiveness as devotion, revealing an unhealthy fixation that borders on stalking.
scenario: a toxic relationship where one partner surveils the other under the guise of love

title: Born in the U.S.A.
literal: a man's life growing up and serving in the Vietnam War
trueSubject: the disillusionment and struggles of working-class Vietnam veterans
subtext: The song critiques America's treatment of veterans and the hollow patriotism that masks systemic neglect and exploitation.
scenario: a Vietnam veteran reflecting on his traumatic past, economic hardship, and abandonment by the country he served

title: The Sound of Silence
literal: a solitary nighttime reverie about silence and visions
trueSubject: alienation and societal disconnection in the modern age
subtext: The silence represents the unspoken despair and lack of genuine communication in a world dominated by superficiality and mass media.
scenario: a lone individual observing a crowd consumed by empty interactions under artificial light

literal: a nostalgic recollection of youthful nights in 1979
trueSubject: the fleeting, reckless nature of adolescence and disillusionment with adulthood
subtext: The song masks a deep longing for lost innocence and the painful realization that youthful freedom is unsustainable, dissolving into apathy and existential drift.
scenario: a group of teenagers driving aimlessly at dawn, high on life and drugs, confronting the inevitability of growing up and fading away

title: Boulevard of Broken Dreams
literal: a solitary walk down an empty street
trueSubject: existential loneliness and emotional isolation
subtext: The narrator is grappling with inner emptiness and a longing for connection, masking despair with a facade of self-sufficiency.
scenario: a person wandering urban streets at night, lost in thought and disconnected from others

title: Jolene
subtext: Instrumental — no lyrics to interpret.

title: Hotel California
subtext: Instrumental — no lyrics to interpret.

title: Fast Car
literal: escaping a dead-end life with a partner in a fast car
trueSubject: the cycle of poverty, addiction, and unfulfilled dreams
subtext: The fast car symbolizes fleeting hope for escape, but the relationship and life circumstances mirror the narrator's parents' failures, trapping her in the same patterns.
scenario: a woman reflecting on her life choices while stuck in a stagnant relationship, realizing she has repeated her mother's mistakes

title: Losing My Religion
literal: a person struggling with unrequited feelings
trueSubject: obsession and emotional self-destruction in a one-sided relationship
subtext: The song masks a desperate, almost addictive fixation on someone who may not even be aware of the narrator's suffering, framed as a crisis of faith or sanity.
scenario: a person spiraling in isolation, replaying imagined interactions with their love interest while questioning their own grip on reality

title: Everybody Hurts
literal: a person contemplating giving up on life
trueSubject: depression and suicidal ideation
subtext: The song is a plea to those suffering in silence to persevere, as pain is universal and shared by everyone.
scenario: a late-night moment of despair where someone feels utterly alone and considers ending their life

title: Creep
literal: a self-loathing outburst at an unattainable person
trueSubject: unrequited obsession masking deep self-hatred and body dysmorphia
subtext: The narrator’s fixation on the other person is a projection of his own perceived inadequacies, and his desire for control stems from a fear of abandonment and self-loathing.
scenario: a socially awkward person confronting their crush in a moment of raw, self-destructive honesty

title: No Surprises
literal: a plea for a quiet, uneventful life
trueSubject: existential despair and suicidal ideation
subtext: The song masks a desire for death as a peaceful escape from emotional and societal suffocation.
scenario: a person standing in a garden, contemplating ending their life to escape pain and alienation

title: Karma Police
subtext: Instrumental — no lyrics to interpret.

title: Fake Plastic Trees
literal: a woman tending a fake plant in an artificial environment
trueSubject: the hollowness of modern existence and emotional disconnection
subtext: The song critiques the artificiality of contemporary life and relationships, where people feel trapped in performative roles and long for authenticity but are exhausted by the effort to maintain the facade.
scenario: a couple living in a sterile, consumerist world, emotionally drained by their own and each other's pretenses

title: Fix You
literal: comforting someone in pain
trueSubject: grieving a lost loved one or relationship
subtext: The narrator is helplessly watching someone they care for suffer through irreversible loss, offering support but unable to truly heal their pain.
scenario: a quiet moment of emotional collapse, possibly after a breakup or death, with the narrator holding the grieving person

title: The Scientist
literal: a plea for reconciliation after a breakup
trueSubject: the regret of prioritizing ambition over love
subtext: The narrator realizes that intellectual pursuits and progress cannot replace emotional connection and is haunted by the loss of a relationship he neglected.
scenario: a late-night moment of reflection after a failed relationship, where the narrator replays memories and mistakes

title: Yellow
literal: a person admiring stars and writing a song for someone they love
trueSubject: obsession and self-sacrifice in love
subtext: The speaker equates their love to an all-consuming, almost unhealthy devotion, where they would metaphorically 'bleed themselves dry' for the object of their affection.
scenario: a solitary moment of intense emotional fixation on a lover, framed as cosmic and transcendent

title: Skinny Love
literal: a failing romantic relationship
trueSubject: emotional self-destruction through codependency
subtext: The singer is trapped in a toxic cycle of love and pain, using the relationship as a form of self-punishment and emotional sabotage.
scenario: a late-night confrontation in a decaying domestic space

title: Holocene
literal: a fragmented recollection of past moments and places
trueSubject: the erosion of self-worth through lost love and nostalgia
subtext: The song masks grief over a failed relationship as a meditation on insignificance, using disjointed memories to symbolize emotional disintegration.
scenario: a late-night drive through a snowstorm, replaying past intimacy in the rearview mirror

title: Ribs
literal: a nostalgic house party with a close friend
trueSubject: the loss of youthful intimacy and mental freedom
subtext: The song mourns the irreversible passage of time and the fading of the unselfconscious connection and creativity once shared with a friend.
scenario: two former best friends lying awake in a childhood bedroom, drunk and haunted by memories of their younger selves

title: Liability
literal: a person reflecting on being too intense for others
trueSubject: self-destructive emotional dependency and codependency
subtext: The narrator’s overwhelming need for love and validation burns out relationships, masking deep loneliness and fear of abandonment as self-sabotage.
scenario: a late-night taxi ride after a breakup, followed by a hollow attempt at intimacy with a partner who tolerates but doesn’t truly connect

title: Tennis Court
literal: a conversation about boredom and superficial success
trueSubject: the emptiness of early fame and performative detachment
subtext: The song masks deep insecurity and disillusionment with the hollow thrill of celebrity by feigning indifference and irony.
scenario: a young starlet on the verge of global fame, alone in a hotel room or backstage, rehearsing a facade of carefree rebellion with a close friend or lover

title: Yesterday
literal: a man mourning the loss of a past relationship
trueSubject: grief and regret over a failed romantic relationship
subtext: The narrator is consumed by nostalgia and self-blame for a love that slipped away, unable to move forward.
scenario: a solitary moment of reflection after a breakup, haunted by memories of happier times

title: Eleanor Rigby
literal: two isolated individuals in a community
trueSubject: social alienation and existential loneliness
subtext: Modern life strips people of connection, leaving them to wither unseen even in shared spaces like churches.
scenario: a quiet village where two strangers live parallel lives of quiet despair, unnoticed until death

title: Blackbird
subtext: Instrumental — no lyrics to interpret.

title: Norwegian Wood (This Bird Has Flown)
literal: a man visiting a woman's apartment and spending the night
trueSubject: unrequited love and emotional detachment
subtext: The narrator reflects on a fleeting, superficial encounter that leaves him feeling used and emotionally abandoned, masking his vulnerability with cold detachment.
scenario: a one-night stand or casual affair ending in loneliness and quiet resentment

title: Help!
literal: a plea for assistance from someone
trueSubject: struggling with sudden fame and personal insecurity
subtext: The song masks the artist's vulnerability and loss of autonomy under the weight of celebrity with a seemingly simple cry for help.
scenario: a young musician overwhelmed by rapid success, longing for stability and emotional support

title: Heroes
literal: a couple imagining themselves as royalty and heroes for a brief moment
trueSubject: fleeting defiance and love amidst oppression or war
subtext: The song captures the desperate, temporary escape of two people finding love and heroism in a moment of chaos, knowing their reality is fragile and doomed.
scenario: two lovers embracing under gunfire during a conflict or uprising, clinging to a brief illusion of safety and grandeur

title: Space Oddity
subtext: Instrumental — no lyrics to interpret.

title: Ashes To Ashes
subtext: Instrumental — no lyrics to interpret.

title: Rocket Man
literal: an astronaut leaving Earth for space
trueSubject: alienation and emotional burnout in a high-pressure career
subtext: The narrator feels disconnected from his personal life and identity due to the demands of his job, which has consumed him to the point of emotional exhaustion.
scenario: a man in a high-stakes profession struggling with isolation and the loss of his true self

title: Tiny Dancer
subtext: Instrumental — no lyrics to interpret.

title: Rehab
literal: refusing to attend rehabilitation
trueSubject: self-destructive addiction and denial
subtext: The song masks a deep struggle with substance abuse behind defiance and humor, revealing fear of losing love and control.
scenario: a person resisting intervention while secretly grappling with dependency and emotional pain

title: Back To Black
literal: a breakup with lingering emotional pain
trueSubject: addiction and codependency in a toxic relationship
subtext: The song masks the agony of loving someone whose addiction (to drugs and another woman) eclipses their love for her, leaving her emotionally shattered and reverting to self-destructive habits.
scenario: a woman confronting her ex-lover’s relapse into substance abuse and infidelity while grappling with her own relapse into grief and self-harm

title: Running Up That Hill
literal: a plea to trade places with a lover to understand each other's pain
trueSubject: gendered power imbalances and emotional disconnect in a relationship
subtext: The speaker longs to experience life through their partner's perspective to bridge the gap caused by systemic and personal misunderstandings.
scenario: a couple in emotional turmoil, where one partner feels unheard and powerless due to societal roles

title: Wuthering Heights
literal: a ghostly visitation from Cathy to Heathcliff at Wuthering Heights
trueSubject: obsessive love and the haunting power of past relationships
subtext: The song embodies the inescapable grip of a toxic, all-consuming love that transcends death and reason.
scenario: Cathy’s spirit pleading with Heathcliff to reunite with her beyond the grave, trapped in a cycle of longing and possession

title: Don't Fear the Reaper
subtext: Instrumental — no lyrics to interpret.

title: Paint It Black
subtext: Instrumental — no lyrics to interpret.

title: Sympathy for the Devil
literal: a charismatic figure boasting of historical atrocities
trueSubject: the banality and ubiquity of human evil
subtext: The devil is not an external force but the collective shadow of humanity’s own cruelty and complicity in violence.
scenario: a seductive narrator recounting centuries of human suffering while implicating the listener in the cycle of destruction

title: Landslide
literal: a person reflecting on change and fear while observing a mountain landscape
trueSubject: fear of personal growth and the inevitability of change in a dependent relationship
subtext: The singer grapples with the terror of losing stability and identity as life and relationships evolve beyond their control.
scenario: a moment of introspection during a solitary mountain climb, confronting aging and emotional reliance

title: Mad World
literal: a bleak, repetitive daily existence
trueSubject: existential depression and alienation
subtext: The singer feels trapped in a meaningless cycle of life, where even death in dreams offers more fulfillment than reality.
scenario: an outsider observing and suffering within a monotonous, emotionally numb society

title: People Are Strange
literal: observations of alienation and isolation
trueSubject: existential loneliness and societal rejection
subtext: The song reflects the distorted perception of reality when one feels ostracized, suggesting that isolation warps how the world is perceived.
scenario: a person wandering through a city feeling invisible and disconnected

title: Riders On the Storm
literal: a journey through a storm with a dangerous presence
trueSubject: existential dread and the inevitability of death
subtext: The storm symbolizes life's chaos and unpredictability, while the killer represents mortality lurking beneath the surface of existence.
scenario: a surreal, rain-soaked highway where fate and danger intertwine

title: Where Is My Mind?
literal: a surreal, disorienting experience with swimming imagery
trueSubject: existential dissociation or mental instability
subtext: The song reflects a fragmented sense of self, questioning reality and the stability of one's own mind, possibly tied to trauma or psychological distress.
scenario: a moment of cognitive dissonance or breakdown, visualized through disjointed, dreamlike imagery

title: Bitter Sweet Symphony - Remastered 2016
literal: a struggle to change within the constraints of life
trueSubject: existential despair and societal alienation
subtext: The song masks a deep sense of powerlessness under societal pressures and the futility of personal transformation.
scenario: a person trapped in the cycle of modern life, seeking escape through music but finding only emptiness

title: Mad World
literal: a monotonous, empty daily routine
trueSubject: existential depression and emotional detachment
subtext: The narrator is trapped in a cycle of despair, finding fleeting comfort only in the escape of dreams or death, while observing the hollow lives of those around them.
scenario: a person standing at the edge of a crowded, indifferent world, contemplating suicide or emotional numbness

title: Everybody Wants To Rule The World
literal: a call to power and control over one's life
trueSubject: the corrupting nature of power and human ambition
subtext: The song critiques the insatiable desire for dominance and the inevitable destruction it brings, masking a plea for restraint and humility.
scenario: a world on the brink of collapse due to unchecked ambition, with the narrator observing the fallout

title: Life On Mars?
literal: a surreal film about a disillusioned girl and chaotic societal scenes
trueSubject: the absurdity and disillusionment of modern civilization and media
subtext: Society is a grotesque, repetitive spectacle that numbs individuals into passive acceptance of its madness.
scenario: a dystopian theater where humanity is trapped in a loop of violence, consumerism, and hollow entertainment

title: Such Great Heights
literal: a long-distance romantic relationship
trueSubject: the idealization and isolation of love in separation
subtext: The song masks the pain of physical distance with an almost obsessive fantasy of spiritual and physical alignment, revealing a reluctance to confront the imperfections of reality.
scenario: one partner on tour, the other at home, clinging to a song as a lifeline while resisting the pressure to rejoin the mundane world

title: Lust For Life
literal: a hedonistic, self-destructive lifestyle fueled by excess
trueSubject: the cyclical trap of addiction and self-sabotage disguised as liberation
subtext: The 'lust for life' is a hollow justification for indulging in substances and fleeting pleasures that ultimately enslave rather than free the narrator.
scenario: a disillusioned outsider oscillating between euphoria and despair in a seedy, exploitative underworld

title: The Passenger
literal: a person riding through a city at night
trueSubject: existential detachment and shared human experience
subtext: The song explores the feeling of observing life as an outsider, where everything feels transient yet universally connected to all people.
scenario: a late-night drive through an urban landscape, viewing the world as both alien and intimately familiar

title: Bitter Sweet Symphony - Remastered 2016
literal: a struggle to break free from life's constraints
trueSubject: existential despair and societal entrapment
subtext: The song masks a deep sense of powerlessness under the guise of a relentless, inescapable cycle of modern life and identity fragmentation.
scenario: a person trapped in the monotony of survival, grappling with the illusion of change while confronting their own stagnation

title: Bring Me To Life
literal: a plea to be awakened from emotional numbness
trueSubject: depression and existential despair
subtext: The singer is trapped in a state of emotional paralysis, equating their existence to a lifeless void that only another person can revive.
scenario: a person in the depths of depression reaching out for salvation from someone they perceive as their only hope

title: Bitter Sweet Symphony - Remastered 2016
literal: a struggle to break free from life's constraints
trueSubject: existential despair and societal entrapment
subtext: The song masks a deep sense of powerlessness under systemic pressures, framing personal identity as an inescapable cycle of suffering and fleeting relief.
scenario: a late-night moment of crisis where the narrator confronts their inability to escape their circumstances despite desperate attempts at change

title: Bitter Sweet Symphony - Remastered 2016
literal: a struggle to change within the constraints of life
trueSubject: existential despair and the illusion of free will under systemic oppression
subtext: The song masks a critique of capitalism and societal conditioning as an inescapable cycle of suffering, where personal agency is an illusion.
scenario: a person trapped in the monotony of survival, seeking solace in music but realizing even that is fleeting

title: Smells Like Teen Spirit
literal: a chaotic, rebellious gathering with nonsensical chants
trueSubject: teenage disillusionment and societal alienation
subtext: The song captures the frustration, apathy, and self-loathing of a generation feeling trapped by expectations and numb to the world around them.
scenario: a disaffected youth in a crowded, dimly lit room, surrounded by peers who are equally lost and desperate for meaning or distraction

title: Come As You Are
literal: an invitation to meet someone as they are or were
trueSubject: the struggle with identity and societal expectations in the face of self-destruction
subtext: The song masks a plea for authenticity while grappling with inner turmoil, possibly tied to addiction, depression, or the pressure of fame.
scenario: a moment of fragile connection amid personal chaos, where the speaker oscillates between acceptance and denial of their own pain

title: Exit Music (For a Film)
literal: a couple fleeing a repressive home
trueSubject: escaping an oppressive authority figure or system
subtext: The song symbolizes a desperate, final act of defiance against a controlling force, possibly leading to a tragic or irreversible outcome.
scenario: two lovers making a night-time escape from a domineering parental or societal figure, possibly facing dire consequences

title: Teardrop
literal: a teardrop falling into fire and poetic imagery of love as action
trueSubject: grief and the fragility of life after loss
subtext: The song masks mourning as a meditation on love, using fire and water to symbolize destruction and memory, with the final lines revealing the subject is someone left behind in the aftermath of death.
scenario: a person standing alone in a dimly lit room, watching a single teardrop fall into a candle flame while recalling the last breath of a loved one who has died

title: Glory Box
literal: a plea for emotional reciprocation in a romantic relationship
trueSubject: female sexual and emotional autonomy within patriarchal constraints
subtext: The speaker is exhausted by performative seduction and demands genuine intimacy as the foundation for equality and mutual vulnerability.
scenario: a woman confronting her lover in a dimly lit room, voice trembling between exhaustion and quiet fury, after years of one-sided emotional labor

title: Roads
literal: a plea for recognition of an unseen struggle
trueSubject: existential alienation and emotional isolation
subtext: The speaker is trapped in a private battle with despair, feeling abandoned by others and unable to reconcile their inner turmoil with the indifferent world around them.
scenario: a person standing alone at dawn, overwhelmed by a sense of futility and betrayal

title: Re:Stacks
literal: a man reflecting on financial and emotional ruin
trueSubject: addiction and self-destruction through gambling or substance abuse
subtext: The song masks a spiral of compulsive behavior and emotional detachment behind metaphors of money, physical strain, and existential emptiness.
scenario: a solitary figure in a decaying home, haunted by loss and the weight of his own choices, possibly after a binge or relapse

title: The Night We Met
literal: a plea to relive a past romantic encounter
trueSubject: grieving a lost love and unresolved emotional debt
subtext: The narrator is trapped in regret and self-blame, using the memory of the night they met as a metaphor for the moment their relationship began to unravel, possibly due to their own actions or inaction.
scenario: a solitary figure reflecting on a failed relationship, haunted by the emotional weight of what was lost and the fear of never finding direction again

title: Ends of the Earth
literal: a traveler urging a lover to join an endless journey
trueSubject: the fear of emotional stagnation and the need for personal freedom
subtext: The speaker equates love with confinement and would rather risk loneliness than sacrifice their autonomy or sense of purpose.
scenario: a restless person standing at a crossroads, torn between commitment and self-discovery

title: Oblivion
literal: a person avoiding walking alone at night due to fear of unseen danger
trueSubject: paranoia and the need for emotional support in mental health struggles
subtext: The song masks deep anxiety and a cry for validation as the speaker grapples with unseen internal threats and isolation.
scenario: a solitary figure trapped in a cycle of fear, longing for reassurance while haunted by the possibility of harm
